heading: Administration, protection, and development
label: 410w Administration, protection, and development
text: When established pursuant to this subchapter, the park shall be administered, protected, and developed by the Secretary of the Interior in accordance with the provisions of the Act of (; ), as amended and supplemented, and the Historic Sites Act of (; U.S.C. 461–467).August 25, 1916August 21, 193539 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1–4149 Stat. 66621See References in Text note below.1So in original.2
notes: References in TextThe Act of (; ), referred to in text, is , , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. L. 113–287128 Stat. 3094section 100101 of Title 54The Historic Sites Act of (; U.S.C. 461–467), referred to in text, is , , also known as the Historic Sites Act of 1935 and as the Historic Sites, Buildings, and Antiquities Act, which enacted sections 461 to 467 of this title. The Act was repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and sections 102303 and 102304 and chapter 3201 of Title 54, National Park Service and Related Programs, by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272.
